headlineSummary: HN sentiment was mixed overall: OpenAI drew a supportive safety note [E3], Anthropic split between praise for Claude Code and criticism of its runtime and speed [E1][E4][E5][E6], and Google Gemini got limited evidence with one sharp complaint and one narrow endorsement [E2][E7].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini looked mixed with limited evidence: one comment praised direct Gemini API calls while another strongly criticized Google’s AI tooling and token counting flow [E2][E7].

Anthropic stayed mixed, as Claude Code was praised for being the best option and useful for rebuilding apps, but also criticized for Bun/security choices and sluggishness in one workflow [E1][E4][E5][E6].
OpenAI came across positively on Codex safety, with a comment saying its sandboxing keeps the agent useful [E3].

evidence.excerpt: So far Claude Code seems best despite its bugs given the generous subscription

annotations.rationale: The commenter rates Claude Code as the best option overall, while noting some bugs.

evidence.excerpt: Google doesn't even provide a tokenizer to count tokens locally. The results of this stupidity can be seen directly in AI studio which makes an API call to count_tokens every time you type in the prompt box.

annotations.rationale: The comment strongly criticizes Google's AI tooling, calling the lack of a local tokenizer 'stupidity' and citing AI Studio as evidence.

evidence.excerpt: At least for Codex, the agent runs commands inside an OS-provided sandbox (Seatbelt on macOS, and other stuff on other platforms). It does not end up "making the agent mostly useless".

annotations.rationale: The commenter defends Codex's sandboxing as a meaningful safety measure that still preserves usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: This makes it even more baffling why anthropic went with bun, a runtime without any sandboxing or security architecture and will rely in apple seatbelt alone?

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes Anthropic's runtime choice as a poor security decision.

evidence.excerpt: Tried to make the jump further to Claude Code like everyone tells me to, but it just doesn't feel right... It may be due to the size of my codebase -- I'm 6 months into solo developer bootstrap startup, so there isn't all that much there, and I can iterate very quickly with Cursor. And it's mostly SPA browser click-tested stuff. Comparatively it feels like Claude Code spends an eternity to do something.

annotations.rationale: Although the commenter hedges that context may matter, they clearly say Claude Code feels too slow and 'doesn't feel right' for their workflow.

evidence.excerpt: To be honest, when I see many vibecoded apps, I just build my own duplicate with Claude Code. It's not that useful to use someone else's vibecode. The idea is enough, or the evidence that it works for someone else means I can just build it myself with Claude Code and I can make it specific to my needs.

annotations.rationale: The commenter presents Claude Code as capable enough to quickly recreate and customize apps for personal needs.

evidence.excerpt: I have been rather unimpressed with Google's Ai products, save direct API calls to gemini

annotations.rationale: Despite criticizing Google's AI products broadly, the commenter explicitly exempts direct API calls to Gemini, implying a favorable view of that offering.
